Gauging accuracy SPC-controlled to prevent over/under-preload. No safety impact; premium refinement and high-torque durability maintained while removing assembly rework.

title: CV joint boot material switch to TPE with crimpless clamping
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace chloroprene (CR) rubber CV boots with thermoplastic-elastomer (TPE/Hytrel) boots and integrated snap clamping. Cuts boot cost and crimp tooling across all powertrain half shafts while sustaining articulation, ozone resistance and grease retention for off-road and high-torque duty.

ideaData.title: CV joint boot material switch to TPE with crimpless clamping
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline outboard/inboard boots are chloroprene rubber held by steel ear clamps requiring crimp tooling. Switch to injection-moulded TPE (Hytrel/TPC-ET) convoluted boots with integral snap-fit or low-profile band, improving fatigue at high articulation and ozone/heat resistance to 150 °C. Alternatives: TPE (TPC-ET), TPV (EPDM-PP), and silicone boots evaluated; grease compatibility re-verified. Convolute count and wall optimised for plunge stroke and 45 deg articulation off-road. Boot fatigue life and grease retention validated under high-torque tow and sustained BEV plunge churn to protect joint durability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Injection-moulded TPE with snap clamp replaces CR rubber moulding plus steel ear-clamp crimping; alternative band-clamp versus integral retainer assessed. Removes crimp tooling and a clamp SKU, shortening assembly. TPE recyclability aids scrap value. New injection tool is the main spend; cycle time lower than rubber cure.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: TPE boot with snap clamp cuts material and crimp tooling cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: TPE boot fatigue validated at full articulation and plunge stroke to protect joint durability off-road and under high-torque tow. Ozone, heat and grease compatibility qualified to 150 °C to prevent cracking and grease loss. Snap/band clamp retention tested against burst and thermal cycling for safety against grease leakage. NVH unaffected. Recyclable TPE improves end-of-life. Premium durability and off-road articulation preserved across all powertrains while removing crimp tooling.

title: Rear differential ring-gear bolt count reduction via flange redesign
description: [ICE,MHEV] Reduce rear differential ring-gear-to-case bolt count from twelve to ten via a stiffened flange and higher-grade fasteners. Cuts fastener cost and assembly torque-down time while maintaining clamp load and joint fatigue under high-torque crawl and tow on the luxury SUV.

ideaData.title: Rear differential ring-gear bolt count reduction via flange redesign
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline hypoid ring gear bolts to the differential case with twelve M10 grade-10.9 fasteners carrying ~3500 Nm crown torque. A stiffened, locally thickened flange with optimised bolt-circle redistributes clamp load, allowing ten M10 grade-12.9 fasteners while raising per-bolt clamp margin. Alternatives: grade-10.9, grade-12.9, and flow-drilled boss with M11 evaluated. FE bolt-pattern analysis confirms flange separation and bearing-bore distortion within limits. Joint fatigue and ring-gear runout validated under crawl, tow and shock reversals to protect mesh accuracy and gear-whine NVH.
ideaData.materialGrade: Grade-12.9 fasteners / GJS-600-3 flange / 18CrNiMo7-6 ring gear
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Two fewer tapped holes and bolts per diff; alternative weld-on versus bolt-on ring gear assessed. Removes two drill/tap operations and two torque-down cycles, cutting machining and assembly time. Higher-grade fastener slightly raises unit price but net BOM falls. Existing case tooling minor revision; torque-station program updated.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Fewer, higher-grade bolts cut fastener cost and assembly time.

ideaData.riskNotes: Reduced bolt count with stiffened flange validated by FE and bolt-load testing to maintain clamp load, preventing flange separation and ring-gear runout that cause gear whine. Joint fatigue qualified under crawl, tow and shock-reversal loads to protect durability and safety of the drive path. Bearing-bore distortion held within limits to preserve L10 life. Torque-to-yield strategy verified. Premium NVH refinement and high-torque off-road durability maintained.

title: Prop-shaft NVH tuned damper deletion via tube wall tuning
description: [ICE,MHEV] Internal cardboard/rubber prop-shaft NVH damper is deleted by tuning tube wall thickness and adding a constrained-layer damping insert only where needed. Removes a damper part and assembly step while controlling driveline boom and bending modes on the high-torque SUV prop shaft.

ideaData.title: Prop-shaft NVH tuned damper deletion via tube wall tuning
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline steel prop tube carries an internal liner/dynamic damper to suppress shell-mode boom and bending resonance under ~3000 Nm. Re-tune tube diameter and wall to push shell and first bending modes outside the excitation band, adding a localised constrained-layer damping (CLD) patch only if residual response remains. Alternatives: tuned-mass internal damper, CLD viscoelastic patch, and cardboard liner retained as fallback. Modal analysis confirms boom frequencies shift above operating range. Torsional capacity and balance preserved; gear-mesh and U-joint orders managed to protect premium cabin refinement.
ideaData.materialGrade: Steel tube wall-tuned / CLD viscoelastic patch
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Wall-tuned tube with optional CLD patch replaces inserted internal damper; alternative applied external mass damper assessed. Removes a damper insertion station and a part from BOM. Tube draw die revised for new wall; balancing retained. CLD application is a simple bonded step if required, lowering net assembly content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Wall tuning relocates resonances, deleting the internal damper part.

ideaData.riskNotes: Tube wall re-tuning validated by modal analysis and vehicle NVH to shift shell and bending modes outside excitation, preventing driveline boom and preserving premium cabin refinement. Torsional capacity and critical speed re-verified for high-torque tow and off-road shock to protect durability and safety. Optional CLD patch covers residual response. Balance and runout held to spec. No reduction in towing capability; premium NVH and durability targets maintained.

evidenceSources.title: LX prop shaft internal damper teardown
Driveline boom control via tube tuning

title: Half-shaft surface coating switch to thin dense chrome over hard chrome
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Replace hard-chrome plating on half-shaft seal journals with thin-dense-chrome or DLC coating. Cuts plating thickness, masking and finishing while maintaining seal-journal wear and corrosion resistance for the half shafts across ICE, MHEV and PHEV variants.

ideaData.title: Half-shaft surface coating switch to thin dense chrome over hard chrome
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline half-shaft seal journals carry thick hard-chrome plating requiring grinding/superfinishing and masking. Switch to thin-dense-chromium (TDC) or DLC coating giving uniform 2-8 µm conformal coverage with low friction at the radial-lip seal interface on 4140/41Cr4 shafts. Alternatives: thin-dense chrome, DLC (a-C:H), and nitrocarburising evaluated. Lower thickness removes post-plate grinding and improves corrosion resistance for off-road exposure. Seal-journal wear and surface finish (Ra <0.2 µm) validated for seal life; spline fatigue and shaft torsional capacity unaffected by the thinner coating under high-torque duty.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Thin conformal TDC/DLC replaces thick hard-chrome plus post-grind; alternative ferritic nitrocarburising assessed. Removes a masking and grinding operation, cutting finishing cycle ~20% and plating chemistry use. Coating-line tooling revised; spline rolling and induction hardening unchanged. Reduced chrome chemistry aids environmental compliance.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Thinner conformal coating deletes post-plate grinding and masking.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thin-dense-chrome/DLC seal-journal wear and corrosion resistance validated for off-road exposure and long seal life, protecting durability. Surface finish held to Ra <0.2 µm to prevent seal-lip wear and oil leakage. Coating adhesion qualified under torsional flex to avoid spalling on the safety-critical shaft. Spline fatigue and torsional capacity unaffected. Hexavalent-chrome reduction aids compliance. Premium durability and refinement preserved across ICE, MHEV and PHEV half shafts.

evidenceSources.title: TDC vs hard chrome seal-journal wear data

title: PHEV reinforced transfer-case differential housing aluminium re-gating
description: [PHEV] PHEV transfer-case differential housing high-pressure die-cast with conservative gating and high scrap. Re-engineer gating and switch to a vacuum-assisted HPDC process, cutting porosity scrap and machining while retaining stiffness for the reinforced hybrid torque-blend transfer path on the luxury SUV.

ideaData.title: PHEV reinforced transfer-case differential housing aluminium re-gating
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline transfer-case/center-diff housing is AlSi9Cu3 HPDC with high porosity scrap, sized for blended ICE+e-motor input to ~4500 Nm. Re-engineer runner/gating and adopt vacuum-assisted HPDC to cut entrapped-gas porosity, allowing thinner reinforced ribs and lower machining stock while holding bearing-bore stiffness. Alternatives: AlSi10Mg, AlSi9Cu3, and AlMg5Si2Mn (Magsimal) rheocast evaluated. Reduced porosity improves fatigue at the reinforced torque-blend bosses. Bearing-bore roundness and housing modal frequency validated to protect L10 life and avoid gear whine under hybrid launch and crawl torque.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Vacuum-assisted HPDC with re-engineered gating replaces conventional HPDC; alternative squeeze-cast and rheocast assessed. Cuts porosity scrap ~40%, allows leak-tight thinner walls and lowers machining stock ~12%. New gating inserts and vacuum tooling required; assembly unchanged. Reduced rework improves yield and net BOM.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Vacuum HPDC re-gating cuts porosity scrap and machining stock.

ideaData.riskNotes: Vacuum-HPDC porosity reduction validated by CT and burst testing to ensure leak-tight, fatigue-resistant reinforced torque-blend bosses, protecting durability and safety under hybrid launch and crawl. Bearing-bore roundness held to protect L10 life and prevent gear whine. Housing modal frequency kept above driveline boom band. Thinner walls re-validated for stiffness across temperature. Reduced porosity lowers warranty leak risk; premium NVH and PHEV torque durability preserved.

title: BEV e-axle differential ring gear skiving replacing hobbing plus grinding
description: [BEV] BEV e-axle reduction-stage and differential gears cut by hobbing then ground. Adopt power skiving with hard finishing only where needed, cutting cycle time and machine count while sustaining the high-torque, high-speed mesh quality demanded by the quiet BEV e-axle.

ideaData.title: BEV e-axle differential ring gear skiving replacing hobbing plus grinding
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline e-axle reduction and differential gears are hobbed then profile-ground for quality DIN5-6 to suppress whine at e-motor speeds to ~16000 rpm under ~3800 Nm output. Adopt power skiving, which is faster than hobbing for internal/cluster geometry, with targeted hard finishing only on critical flanks. Alternatives: power skiving, gear honing, and continuous generating grinding evaluated; 16MnCr5/20MnCr5 case-hardened gears. Skiving raises throughput and reduces machine footprint. Mesh quality, profile/lead crowning and surface finish validated to hold low e-axle gear whine and tooth-flank Hertzian contact stress for L10 life.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Power skiving plus selective honing replaces hobbing then full grinding; alternative gear honing versus generating grinding compared. Cuts cutting cycle time ~25% and machine count, lowering capital and floor space. Skiving tools are new tooling; case hardening unchanged. Reduced grinding lowers wheel consumption and energy.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Power skiving cuts gear cycle time and machine count for the e-axle.

ideaData.riskNotes: Power-skiving mesh quality and surface finish validated to hold low e-axle gear whine, critical in silent BEV cabins, preserving premium NVH. Profile/lead crowning and Hertzian contact stress qualified for tooth-flank pitting resistance and L10 life under instant torque and high speed, protecting durability. Selective hard finishing covers critical flanks. Tool-wear SPC controls geometry drift. No safety impact; high-torque BEV durability and refinement maintained at lower machining cost.

evidenceSources.title: Power skiving e-axle gear throughput study

title: Prop-shaft center bearing rubber isolator material and bracket simplification
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Center support bearing rubber isolator and stamped bracket are simplified to a single overmoulded NR/SBR isolator with integrated bracket features. Cuts parts and assembly while preserving isolation of bending modes and driveline boom on two-piece prop shafts across powertrains.

ideaData.title: Prop-shaft center bearing rubber isolator material and bracket simplification
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline center support bearing uses a natural-rubber isolator, a sealed ball bearing and a multi-piece stamped bracket to isolate bending-mode energy from the body under ~3000 Nm. Consolidate to an overmoulded NR/SBR isolator with integral mounting features and a single-piece bracket, tuning radial/axial rates to retain isolation. Alternatives: NR, SBR, and hydraulically-damped isolator evaluated. Dynamic stiffness and creep validated -40 to 120 °C. Bearing L10 life and isolation of driveline boom preserved to protect premium refinement under high-torque launch and off-road shock.
ideaData.materialGrade: Overmoulded NR/SBR isolator / hydraulic isolator option
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Overmoulded isolator with integral bracket replaces separate isolator plus multi-piece stamping; alternative bonded versus mechanical retention assessed. Removes stamping and a fastener set, cutting BOM and assembly. New overmould tool is tooling; bearing press-fit unchanged. Fewer interfaces lower line complexity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Overmoulded integral isolator deletes stampings and fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: Isolator dynamic stiffness and creep validated -40 to 120 °C to maintain isolation of bending modes and driveline boom, preserving premium cabin refinement. Bearing L10 life protected under high-torque launch and off-road shock for durability. Overmould bond strength qualified against fatigue and thermal cycling for the safety-relevant support. Tuned rates match baseline NVH. Integral bracket stiffness verified; premium refinement and durability maintained across ICE, MHEV and PHEV prop shafts.

evidenceSources.title: Center bearing isolator rate tuning data

title: Differential side-gear thrust washer to low-friction polymer bearing
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace steel differential side-gear and pinion thrust washers with low-friction PEEK-composite thrust bearings. Cuts material cost and parasitic drag across all powertrain differentials while sustaining contact pressure and wear under high-torque crawl, tow and instant-torque BEV loads.

ideaData.title: Differential side-gear thrust washer to low-friction polymer bearing
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline differential side-gear and pinion-gear back faces ride on hardened steel thrust washers, generating friction and requiring grinding. Replace with PEEK/PTFE-composite thrust bearings (or bronze-backed PTFE) lowering coefficient and parasitic drag while spreading contact pressure under separating loads from ~3500-4500 Nm crown torque. Alternatives: PEEK-composite, bronze-backed PTFE, and DU dry-bearing evaluated. PV rating and wear validated for sustained crawl, tow and instant BEV torque. Reduced drag aids efficiency; backlash and side-gear float controlled to protect mesh and gear-whine NVH.
ideaData.materialGrade: PEEK/PTFE composite washer / bronze-backed PTFE / DU bearing
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Stamped/moulded polymer composite washer replaces ground steel washer; alternative injection-moulded versus skived-PTFE tape assessed. Removes a grinding operation and lowers material cost. PV-rated polymer needs no hardening. New blanking/moulding tool is tooling; assembly stack unchanged with revised thickness selection.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Polymer thrust bearing cuts cost, grinding and parasitic drag.

ideaData.riskNotes: Polymer thrust-bearing PV rating and wear validated under separating loads from crawl, tow and instant BEV torque to protect durability. Side-gear float and backlash controlled to preserve mesh accuracy and prevent gear whine. Thermal limit verified for sustained high-torque oil temperature. Reduced parasitic drag aids efficiency without compromising lock-up. Fluid-additive compatibility qualified. No safety impact; premium NVH and high-torque durability preserved across all powertrain differentials.

evidenceSources.title: Polymer thrust bearing PV wear data

title: MHEV half-shaft commonisation left-right via symmetric intermediate shaft
description: [MHEV] Eliminate the asymmetric long/short half-shaft pair on the 48V SUV by adopting a symmetric layout with a supported intermediate shaft. Lets left and right outer shafts share one part number, cutting SKUs and tooling while controlling torque steer and shaft NVH.

ideaData.title: MHEV half-shaft commonisation left-right via symmetric intermediate shaft
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV. Baseline front drive uses unequal-length left/right half shafts with a unique long shaft, complicating SKUs and inducing torque steer. Add a bearing-supported intermediate shaft so both outer drive shafts become equal length and share one part number, transmitting ~2400 Nm per side with 48V launch assist. Alternatives: symmetric intermediate-shaft layout, unequal-length retained, and hollow equal shafts evaluated; 4140/41Cr4 shafts. Equal lengths balance plunge and reduce torque steer. Intermediate-shaft bearing L10 and shaft bending mode validated to protect durability and avoid driveline boom.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Symmetric layout lets one outer shaft PN serve both sides; alternative make-vs-buy intermediate shaft assessed. Halves outer half-shaft SKUs and tooling, simplifying service and inventory. Adds an intermediate-shaft bearing and bracket to BOM but nets lower complexity. Spline rolling and induction hardening share one line.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Symmetric layout commonises outer shafts and cuts SKUs/tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Equal-length outer shafts validated to balance plunge force and cut torque steer, improving on-road refinement and steering feel. Intermediate-shaft support bearing L10 life qualified for 48V launch-assist torque to protect durability. Shaft bending mode kept above excitation to avoid driveline boom. Spline fatigue unchanged. Added bearing reliability assessed for warranty. Premium NVH, MHEV efficiency and durability preserved while commonising the safety-critical drive shafts.

title: BEV e-axle differential housing magnesium cover replacing aluminium
description: [BEV] Replace the aluminium e-axle differential/gearbox cover with an AM60B magnesium die-casting. Removes mass from the BEV e-axle for range while maintaining oil-tight sealing, bearing-bore stiffness and NVH containment for the high-torque differential of the luxury SUV.

ideaData.title: BEV e-axle differential housing magnesium cover replacing aluminium
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline e-axle differential/reduction cover is AlSi10Mg, sealing oil and supporting an output-bearing bore under ~3800 Nm. Switch to AM60B magnesium die-casting, ~30% lighter at equal stiffness via ribbing, trimming e-axle mass for range. Alternatives: AM60B, AZ91D magnesium, and AlSi10Mg retained as baseline; AE44 creep-resistant Mg for hot zones. Galvanic isolation and coating specified at steel-bearing and fastener interfaces. Cover stiffness, bearing-bore roundness and sealing validated -40 to 150 °C to protect L10 life, prevent oil weep and contain e-axle gear whine in the silent BEV cabin.
ideaData.materialGrade: AM60B Mg cover / AZ91D / AlSi10Mg baseline
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Magnesium HPDC replaces aluminium HPDC; alternative thixomoulding assessed. Lower density cuts mass cost per part and machining loads; faster solidification shortens cycle. Galvanic-isolation coating and stainless fasteners add cost. New Mg-rated die and hot-chamber tooling required; sealing-face machining unchanged.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Magnesium cover removes e-axle mass aiding BEV range at equal stiffness.

ideaData.riskNotes: Magnesium cover stiffness and bearing-bore roundness validated to protect output-bearing L10 life under instant BEV torque. Galvanic corrosion controlled by coating and isolation at steel interfaces to safeguard durability and sealing. Oil-tightness and creep qualified -40 to 150 °C to prevent weep. Cover modal frequency and wall damping tuned to contain e-axle gear whine prominent in silent BEV cabins, preserving premium NVH. Flammability and repair handled per Mg process controls; durability maintained.

title: Rear differential pinion bearing tandem angular-contact replacing tapered pair
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Replace the tapered-roller pinion bearing pair with a matched tandem angular-contact ball-bearing set on the rear differential. Cuts bearing cost and parasitic drag while sustaining pinion overhung-load capacity and L10 life under high-torque tow and crawl on the SUV.

ideaData.title: Rear differential pinion bearing tandem angular-contact replacing tapered pair
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline hypoid pinion is straddle/overhung-mounted on a tapered-roller pair set to high preload, generating drag and needing precise shim setting under ~3500 Nm. Replace with a matched tandem angular-contact ball-bearing set giving lower friction and adequate combined radial/thrust capacity for the pinion overhung load. Alternatives: tapered-roller pair, tandem angular-contact ball, and cylindrical-plus-4-point combinations evaluated; 100Cr6 rings. Preload simplified to ground-spacer setting. Pinion deflection, bearing L10 life and gear-mesh pattern validated under tow and crawl to protect durability and limit gear whine.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tandem angular-contact set with ground spacer replaces shimmed tapered pair; alternative cartridge versus loose-bearing build assessed. Lowers bearing unit cost and simplifies preload setting, cutting assembly time. Bore and seal machining unchanged. Spacer grinding is minor tooling; reduced drag aids efficiency.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Angular-contact set lowers bearing cost, drag and preload complexity.

ideaData.riskNotes: Tandem angular-contact capacity validated for pinion overhung radial/thrust load under high-torque tow and crawl to protect L10 life and durability. Pinion deflection and gear-mesh pattern qualified to maintain hypoid contact and limit gear whine. Ground-spacer preload setting verified for repeatability and thermal stability. Lower friction reduces drag without sacrificing stiffness. Off-road shock capacity confirmed. Premium NVH refinement and high-torque durability preserved.

title: BEV equal-length lightweight half shafts with hollow tripod trunnions
description: [BEV] BEV e-axle lightweight half shafts adopt hollow flow-formed bars plus hollow-bored tripod trunnions and compact outer joints. Strips rotating and unsprung mass from the e-axle for range and ride while sustaining instant dual-motor torque and regen reversals on the luxury SUV.

ideaData.title: BEV equal-length lightweight half shafts with hollow tripod trunnions
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline e-axle half shafts use solid bars and solid tripod trunnions carrying ~2600 Nm per side from instant dual-motor torque. Combine hollow flow-formed shaft bars, hollow-bored tripod trunnions and a compact high-articulation outer Rzeppa joint to cut rotating/unsprung mass ~1.3 kg per side for range and ride. Alternatives: 34CrMo4/4140 tube, 4340 high-strength, and CFRP shaft evaluated; 100Cr6 rollers, 16MnCr5 case-hardened trunnions. Torsional capacity, spline fatigue and roller L10 validated for instant torque and regen reversals. Shaft bending mode and joint NVH controlled for the silent BEV cabin.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Flow-forming, trunnion gun-drilling/boring and compact-joint forging replace solid bar turning and solid trunnions; alternative rotary-swage and net-forged joint assessed. Cuts shaft and joint material BOM ~22% and rotating mass. Friction-weld and boring tooling new; spline rolling and induction hardening shared. Equal lengths simplify SKUs.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hollow shafts and trunnions strip e-axle mass while cutting material cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hollow shaft and trunnion torsional and spline fatigue re-validated for instant BEV torque and regen reversals to protect safety-critical durability. Roller L10 life at full articulation qualified for ride and off-road. Friction-weld and bored sections burst-tested for safety. Shaft bending mode kept above excitation to avoid whirl; compact-joint NVH tuned for the silent BEV cabin. Reduced unsprung mass aids ride refinement. Premium durability, range and NVH targets maintained.
